# Jasmine Mote, PhD

> Licensed clinical psychologist, Research Assistant Professor at Boston University, and science writer based in Somerville, MA. She sees therapy clients in the Boston area through Cambridge Psychology Group and writes Mental Healthy, a Substack newsletter on the science of mental health for parents.

Her work spans three mutually reinforcing roles: researcher, therapist, and writer. The through-line is a commitment to translating rigorous science into practical understanding of how people feel, connect, and cope.

## Therapy

Jasmine sees adult clients through Cambridge Psychology Group in the Boston area. She is a licensed clinical psychologist specializing in:

- Mental health challenges in pregnancy and postpartum (perinatal mental health)
- Depression and mood difficulties
- Anxiety, OCD, and perfectionism
- Psychosis and serious mental illness
- Life transitions — new parenthood, career change, identity
- Academics, researchers, and high-stress professionals

She practices evidence-based therapy and is affirming of LGBTQIA+ identities and committed to anti-racist practice.

- Therapy page: https://jasminemote.com/therapy/
- Cambridge Psychology Group profile: https://cambridgepsych.org/clinicians/jasmine-mote/
- Psychology Today profile: https://www.psychologytoday.com/us/therapists/jasmine-mote-arlington-ma/1050066

## Research

Research Assistant Professor at Boston University Sargent College, Approach Motivation and Participation (AMP) Lab. Her research focuses on emotion, social motivation, and interpersonal connection — particularly in people living with psychosis and schizophrenia. She has 30+ peer-reviewed publications and 15+ years of active research.

Research areas:
- Emotion and anhedonia in schizophrenia
- Social processes in psychopathology (loneliness, social motivation)
- Mental health intervention development (psychosocial and digital)
- Culture, environment, and mental health (racial health disparities, green space, pet-human bonds)

Methods expertise: ecological momentary assessment (EMA), smartphone sensing, psychophysiology, qualitative methods, experimental design.

Funding: National Institute of Mental Health (NIMH), Eunice Kennedy Shriver NICHD, Waltham Petcare Science Institute, American Psychological Foundation.

- Research page: https://jasminemote.com/research/
- Google Scholar: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=achpZVQAAAAJ&hl=en
- ResearchGate: https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Jasmine-Mote

## Writing

Jasmine writes Mental Healthy, a Substack newsletter on the science of mental health for parents and people navigating real life. She has also written for Scientific American (Science of Parenting series) and other publications.

- Newsletter: https://jasminemote.substack.com
- Writing & Press page: https://jasminemote.com/writing/

## Resources

She maintains a curated resources page for two audiences: people navigating graduate school in clinical psychology (PhD guide), and people in Massachusetts seeking early psychosis and general mental health support.

- Resources page: https://jasminemote.com/resources/

## Contact & Collaboration

For therapy inquiries, prospective clients should use her Cambridge Psychology Group profile. For research collaboration, writing, speaking, or media inquiries, use the contact form on the Work With Me page.

- Work With Me: https://jasminemote.com/work-with-me/
- Bluesky: https://bsky.app/profile/jmote.bsky.social
